# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @products = Product.includes(:category)
      .where("name LIKE :query OR category_name LIKE :query", query: "%#{params[:search][:name]}%")
      .where(category_id: params[:search][:categories]) if params[:search].key?(:categories)
      .where(price: [params[:search][:min_price], params[:search][:max_price]]) if params[:search].key?(:min_price) && params[:search].key?(:max_price)

    @products = @products.order(params[:sort_by]).page(params[:page])

    render json: @products.map(&:to_json), status: :ok
  end

  private

  def products_params
    params.require(:search).permit(:name, categories: [], min_price: :float, max_price: :float)
  end
end
